chief_complaint: Generalized weakness and dizziness with near-fall
reasoning: 79-year-old female presenting with vague symptoms of weakness, dizziness, and fatigue. Reports near-fall getting out of bed but caught herself — no injury. Daughter concerned enough to bring her in. Speech is non-specific and does not suggest acute emergency from verbal presentation alone. However, EHR reveals significant risk amplifiers: immunosuppression on methotrexate and prednisone for rheumatoid arthritis, recent UTI treatment 6 days ago (bounce-back visit), and beta-blocker therapy that may mask tachycardia. These factors elevate from STANDARD to MODERATE priority.
risk_amplifiers_detected: Immunosuppression — methotrexate 15 mg weekly + prednisone 7.5 mg daily
Recent UTI treatment 6 days ago — possible treatment failure / bounce-back
